body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**[Anhedonia in depression].** — Encephale. PMID: 23937895. DOI: 10.1016/j.encep.2013.07.001. Anhedonia, or markedly diminished interest or pleasure, is ahallmark symptom of major depression, schizophrenia, and other neuropsychiatric disorders. The term "anhedonia" was introduced by the French psychologist Ribot in 1896 to describe the counterpart to analgesia in his patients, for which "it was impossible to find the least pleasure".
